decision_tree:
|--- direct_gap_A <= 38.45
|   |--- class: direct
|--- direct_gap_A >  38.45
|   |--- jams_channel <= 0.50
|   |   |--- class: diffusion
|   |--- jams_channel >  0.50
|   |   |--- class: CP
ground_truth_note: GT = 3 multi-signal-validated anchors only; predicted method == method that closes.

fold_observations.Rho_direct: Boltz tiled-MSA M2 6/6 (fwd6/rev0), M1 radius29.4 CV0.02 planar0.5, pLDDT75.5 -> ring closes
fold_observations.gp16_direct: Boltz tiled-MSA M2 5/5 (fwd5/rev0), M1 radius26.8 CV0.02 planar0.2 -> ring ALSO closes, yet gp16-direct is the KNOWN-WRONG method (AF3 0/5; C-term contacts DNA 4A). => apo Boltz-tiled cannot adjudicate method choice; substrate-channel fouling is invisible to a protein-only fold.

caveat: diffusion class has 0 validated members; its one testable in-panel prediction (Rho) is unsupported. apo Boltz-tiled over-closes (gp16-direct 5/5) so it cannot rank methods -> the discriminating signal is the structure-derived substrate-fouling descriptor, cross-checked by AF3/MD/experiment, not a single apo fold.
